name: South America
summary: The continent from which a fanatic wrote a rambling letter to a newspaper editor, deducing a dire future from visions he had seen.
description:
South America appears among Professor Angell's press cuttings from the period of the 1925 disturbances: a rambling letter to the editor of a paper there, in which a fanatic deduced a dire future from visions he had seen.

The item stands in the record beside the other scattered cases of the same period — suicides, unrest, and mass excitements gathered from around the world.

sources.quote: Here likewise a rambling letter to the editor of a paper in South America, where a fanatic deduces a dire future from visions he has seen.
